2026-04-09T20:39:16.2920834Z ◆ The MCP server requires initialization first. It uses SSE (server-sent events) for the response stream. I need to first initialize the session, then call the tool. But the HTTP protocol here uses SSE which is a streaming protocol, so I need to handle this differently.

2026-04-09T20:39:16.2922959Z   
2026-04-09T20:39:16.2923505Z   Looking at the MCP protocol, after initialization you need to send `notifications/initialized` before calling tools. Let me try to do this in sequence.
2026-04-09T20:39:16.2924107Z   
2026-04-09T20:39:16.2924732Z   Actually, looking at the HTTP MCP protocol, it seems each request might be stateless but you need to include session ID in subsequent requests. Let me look at the headers more carefully.

Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). 2026-04-09T20:39:16.4731820Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T20:38:52.36641423Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.Core`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\n**Triggered by:** commit 30f0d27fb (PR #58009) by @m-nash\n\n## What Changed\n\nThis commit merged the Azure.Identity credential configuration schema definitions (`credential`, `chainableCredential`, `tokenCachePersistenceOptions`, `authenticationRecord`, `browserCustomizationOptions`) into the Azure.Core `ConfigurationSchema.json`. This completes the migration of Azure.Identity types into Azure.Core that began in PR #57200 — the credential types themselves moved first, and now their configuration schema definitions follow.\n\nThe merged schema enables IntelliSense and validation in `appsettings.json` for Azure credential configuration, just as the 1.52.0 release added schema support for `Retry` and `Diagnostics` options.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **CHANGELOG `Features Added` is empty for 1.53.0-beta.1**: The credential schema merge (adding `credential`, `chainableCredential`, `tokenCachePersistenceOptions`, `authenticationRecord`, and `browserCustomizationOptions` schema definitions to `ConfigurationSchema.json`) is not listed under `Features Added` — only under `Other Changes`. This is a user-visible feature that enables credential `appsettings.json` IntelliSense.\n- **README has no mention of configuration schema or DI support**: The `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` does not mention the `ConfigurationSchema.json` NuGet-embedded schema (for `appsettings.json` IntelliSense), does not reference the `ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md` guide, and does not reflect that Azure.Identity credential types now live in Azure.Core.\n- **README Key concepts list is outdated**: The Key concepts bullet list mentions `TokenCredentials` abstractly but does not mention that Azure.Identity-originated credential types (`DefaultAzureCredential`, etc.) are now provided by Azure.Core itself, nor the configuration/DI integration (`AddAzureClient`, `WithAzureCredential`).\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n#### 1a. Update `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md`\n\nEdit `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md` — replace the current `1.53.0-beta.1` block:\n\n```markdown\n## 1.53.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\n\n### Features Added\n\n### Breaking Changes\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- `Azure.Identity` credential types are now included in `Azure.Core`, enabling SDK libraries to provide configuration-driven DI extensions (e.g., `AddAzureSecretClient`) without requiring a direct dependency on `Azure.Identity`.\n```\n\nWith:\n\n```markdown\n## 1.53.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Extended the JSON schema embedded in the NuGet package to include Azure.Identity credential configuration definitions (`credential`, `chainableCredential`, `tokenCachePersistenceOptions`, `authenticationRecord`, `browserCustomizationOptions`). This enables IntelliSense and validation for credential configuration in `appsettings.json` when using `AddAzureClient`/`WithAzureCredential` DI extensions.\n\n### Breaking Changes\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- `Azure.Identity` credential types are now included in `Azure.Core`, enabling SDK libraries to provide configuration-driven DI extensions (e.g., `AddAzureSecretClient`) without requiring a direct dependency on `Azure.Identity`.\n```\n\n#### 1b. Update `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md`\n\n**Edit 1** — replace the Key concepts bullet list (lines 24–30):\n\n```markdown\n- Configuring service clients, e.g. configuring retries, logging (`ClientOptions`).\n- Accessing HTTP response details (`Response`, `Response\u003cT\u003e`).\n- Calling long-running operations (`Operation\u003cT\u003e`).\n- Paging and asynchronous streams (`AsyncPageable\u003cT\u003e`).\n- Exceptions for reporting errors from service requests in a consistent fashion. (`RequestFailedException`).\n- Customizing requests (`RequestContext`).\n- Abstractions for representing Azure SDK credentials. (`TokenCredentials`).\n```\n\nWith:\n\n```markdown\n- Configuring service clients, e.g. configuring retries, logging (`ClientOptions`).\n- Accessing HTTP response details (`Response`, `Response\u003cT\u003e`).\n- Calling long-running operations (`Operation\u003cT\u003e`).\n- Paging and asynchronous streams (`AsyncPageable\u003cT\u003e`).\n- Exceptions for reporting errors from service requests in a consistent fashion. (`RequestFailedException`).\n- Customizing requests (`RequestContext`).\n- Abstractions for representing Azure SDK credentials (`TokenCredential`, `DefaultAzureCredential`, and other credential types are now available directly in `Azure.Core`).\n- Configuration-driven client setup and dependency injection via `AddAzureClient`, `WithAzureCredential`, and `appsettings.json` schema support.\n```\n\n**Edit 2** — insert a new sub-section after line 32 (\"Below, you will find sections explaining these shared concepts in more detail.\") and before `### Thread safety`:\n\n```markdown\n### Configuration and Dependency Injection\n\nAzure.Core provides extensions for configuring Azure SDK clients using `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection` and `Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration`:\n\n- `AddAzureClient` / `AddKeyedAzureClient` — register an Azure client in the DI container with settings resolved from `appsettings.json`\n- `WithAzureCredential` — automatically resolve and attach Azure credential from configuration\n- JSON schema embedded in the NuGet package provides IntelliSense and validation for the `AzureClients` section in `appsettings.json`, including `Retry`, `Diagnostics`, and full credential configuration options\n\nFor complete details and examples, see the [Configuration and Dependency Injection](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md) guide.\n\n```\n\n**Edit 3** — update the `Next steps` section to add a link to the DI guide. Replace:\n\n```markdown\n## Next steps\n\nExplore and install [available Azure SDK libraries](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/releases/latest/dotnet.html).\n```\n\nWith:\n\n```markdown\n## Next steps\n\nExplore and install [available Azure SDK libraries](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/releases/latest/dotnet.html).\n\nFor configuration-driven client setup with dependency injection (including credential resolution from `appsettings.json`), see the [Configuration and Dependency Injection](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md) guide.\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nNo new snippet test files are required. The `ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md` doc already contains comprehensive inline examples. No `#region Snippet:` placeholders need to be added to the README for this update.\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README at `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` currently has all required sections. The changes above add documentation for newly available features without removing any existing content.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/core/Azure.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 core`\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 core`\n5. `dotnet format sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\n","title":"[Azure Core] Docs: Update README and CHANGELOG for credential schema merge and DI integration"},"name":"create_issue"}}}

2026-04-09T20:39:16.5053694Z 2026/04/09 20:38:25 Request body: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"create_issue","arguments":{"title":"[Azure Core] Docs: Update README and CHANGELOG for credential schema merge and DI integration","body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.Core`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\n**Triggered by:** commit 30f0d27fb (PR #58009) by @m-nash\n\n## What Changed\n\nThis commit merged the Azure.Identity credential configuration schema definitions (`credential`, `chainableCredential`, `tokenCachePersistenceOptions`, `authenticationRecord`, `browserCustomizationOptions`) into the Azure.Core `ConfigurationSchema.json`. This completes the migration of Azure.Identity types into Azure.Core that began in PR #57200 — the credential types themselves moved first, and now their configuration schema definitions follow.\n\nThe merged schema enables IntelliSense and validation in `appsettings.json` for Azure credential configuration, just as the 1.52.0 release added schema support for `Retry` and `Diagnostics` options.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **CHANGELOG `Features Added` is empty for 1.53.0-beta.1**: The credential schema merge (adding `credential`, `chainableCredential`, `tokenCachePersistenceOptions`, `authenticationRecord`, and `browserCustomizationOptions` schema definitions to `ConfigurationSchema.json`) is not listed under `Features Added` — only under `Other Changes`. This is a user-visible feature that enables credential `appsettings.json` IntelliSense.\n- **README has no mention of configuration schema or DI support**: The `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` does not mention the `ConfigurationSchema.json` NuGet-embedded schema (for `appsettings.json` IntelliSense), does not reference the `ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md` guide, and does not reflect that Azure.Identity credential types now live in Azure.Core.\n- **README Key concepts list is outdated**: The Key concepts bullet list mentions `TokenCredentials` abstractly but does not mention that Azure.Identity-originated credential types (`DefaultAzureCredential`, etc.) are now provided by Azure.Core itself, nor the configuration/DI integration (`AddAzureClient`, `WithAzureCredential`).\n\n<details>\n<summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n#### 1a. Update `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md`\n\nEdit `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md` — replace the current `1.53.0-beta.1` block:\n\n```markdown\n## 1.53.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\n\n### Features Added\n\n### Breaking Changes\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- `Azure.Identity` credential types are now included in `Azure.Core`, enabling SDK libraries to provide configuration-driven DI extensions (e.g., `AddAzureSecretClient`) without requiring a direct dependency on `Azure.Identity`.\n```\n\nWith:\n\n```markdown\n## 1.53.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Extended the JSON schema embedded in the NuGet package to include Azure.Identity credential configuration definitions (`credential`, `chainableCredential`, `tokenCachePersistenceOptions`, `authenticationRecord`, `browserCustomizationOptions`). This enables IntelliSense and validation for credential configuration in `appsettings.json` when using `AddAzureClient`/`WithAzureCredential` DI extensions.\n\n### Breaking Changes\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- `Azure.Identity` credential types are now included in `Azure.Core`, enabling SDK libraries to provide configuration-driven DI extensions (e.g., `AddAzureSecretClient`) without requiring a direct dependency on `Azure.Identity`.\n```\n\n#### 1b. Update `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md`\n\n**Edit 1** — replace the Key concepts bullet list (lines 24–30):\n\n```markdown\n- Configuring service clients, e.g. configuring retries, logging (`ClientOptions`).\n- Accessing HTTP response details (`Response`, `Response<T>`).\n- Calling long-running operations (`Operation<T>`).\n- Paging and asynchronous streams (`AsyncPageable<T>`).\n- Exceptions for reporting errors from service requests in a consistent fashion. (`RequestFailedException`).\n- Customizing requests (`RequestContext`).\n- Abstractions for representing Azure SDK credentials. (`TokenCredentials`).\n```\n\nWith:\n\n```markdown\n- Configuring service clients, e.g. configuring retries, logging (`ClientOptions`).\n- Accessing HTTP response details (`Response`, `Response<T>`).\n- Calling long-running operations (`Operation<T>`).\n- Paging and asynchronous streams (`AsyncPageable<T>`).\n- Exceptions for reporting errors from service requests in a consistent fashion. (`RequestFailedException`).\n- Customizing requests (`RequestContext`).\n- Abstractions for representing Azure SDK credentials (`TokenCredential`, `DefaultAzureCredential`, and other credential types are now available directly in `Azure.Core`).\n- Configuration-driven client setup and dependency injection via `AddAzureClient`, `WithAzureCredential`, and `appsettings.json` schema support.\n```\n\n**Edit 2** — insert a new sub-section after line 32 (\"Below, you will find sections explaining these shared concepts in more detail.\") and before `### Thread safety`:\n\n```markdown\n### Configuration and Dependency Injection\n\nAzure.Core provides extensions for configuring Azure SDK clients using `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection` and `Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration`:\n\n- `AddAzureClient` / `AddKeyedAzureClient` — register an Azure client in the DI container with settings resolved from `appsettings.json`\n- `WithAzureCredential` — automatically resolve and attach Azure credential from configuration\n- JSON schema embedded in the NuGet package provides IntelliSense and validation for the `AzureClients` section in `appsettings.json`, including `Retry`, `Diagnostics`, and full credential configuration options\n\nFor complete details and examples, see the [Configuration and Dependency Injection](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md) guide.\n\n```\n\n**Edit 3** — update the `Next steps` section to add a link to the DI guide. Replace:\n\n```markdown\n## Next steps\n\nExplore and install [available Azure SDK libraries](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/releases/latest/dotnet.html).\n```\n\nWith:\n\n```markdown\n## Next steps\n\nExplore and install [available Azure SDK libraries](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/releases/latest/dotnet.html).\n\nFor configuration-driven client setup with dependency injection (including credential resolution from `appsettings.json`), see the [Configuration and Dependency Injection](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md) guide.\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nNo new snippet test files are required. The `ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md` doc already contains comprehensive inline examples. No `#region Snippet:` placeholders need to be added to the README for this update.\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README at `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` currently has all required sections. The changes above add documentation for newly available features without removing any existing content.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/core/Azure.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 core`\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 core`\n5. `dotnet format sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\n\n</details>\n\n## Next Steps\n\n> [!TIP]\n> **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\n"}}}
